skills.aiInstallPrompt:
Install the skill 'gchat-action-items' by writing the file below.

Universal path: ./skills/gchat-action-items/SKILL.md
Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/gchat-action-items/SKILL.md
Cursor path: ~/.cursor/skills/gchat-action-items/SKILL.md
Claude path: ~/.claude/skills/gchat-action-items/SKILL.md
VS Code agent path: .github/skills/gchat-action-items/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: gchat-action-items
description: >
  Extract tasks from Chat threads. Requires integrations: @google-chat, @notion. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: on-demand. Triggers: "gchat action items", /gchat-action-items.
metadata:
  category: google-chat
  type: combo
  cadence: on-demand
  risk: low
  connectors: google-chat,notion
  integrations: @google-chat,@notion
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gchat-action-items

Extract tasks from Chat threads.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Decisions and commitments made in fast-moving Google Chat threads evaporate by Friday: "can you own the rollout comms?" - "sure" - and nobody wrote it down. Run this after a busy day or a chat-based working session to mine the user's spaces for commitments (theirs and others') and land them in Notion or a copy-ready list.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Which spaces/DMs to scan (named list, or the user's most active spaces) and the window (default: last 24-48h)
- Whose tasks matter: only things assigned to the user (default), things the user assigned to others, or the whole team's
- Target Notion database (name/URL) and its property names - or "just give me the list"
- How to treat vague volunteering ("I can take a look") - include marked as soft, or hard commitments only

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Brief channel-appropriate updates.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-chat** - Google Chat
  - MCP server key: `google-chat`
  - Example tools: `google-chat__list_spaces`, `google-chat__list_messages`

- **@notion** - Notion
  - MCP server key: `notion`
  - Example tools: `notion__query_database`, `notion__create_page`, `notion__update_page`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- Chat scanning is **read-only** - never post, react, or reply in any space while extracting.
- Writes to Notion happen only after the review table and an explicit `apply`.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Chat and Notion tools in this session. List the user's spaces, confirm the scan set, and pull messages for the window - **thread-aware**: a request and its "will do" reply may sit hours apart in the same thread, so read threads whole, not messages in isolation.
2. Detect commitments with chat-specific signals:
   - Direct assignment: "@user can you…", "user, please…" followed by any acknowledgment (including a 👍 or ✅ reaction - in chat culture, a reaction IS acceptance).
   - Self-commitment: "I'll…", "I can take…", "leave it with me".
   - Group decisions with an owner named: "decided: Priya updates the doc".
   - Deadline fragments: "by EOD", "before the demo Thursday" - resolve against the message's timestamp.
3. Apply the noise filter chat needs: exclude banter, hypotheticals ("someone should really…" with no taker), questions that never got an answer, and anything superseded later in the thread ("actually, never mind - I'll handle it").
4. For each surviving item capture: verb-first task, owner, requester, deadline (resolved date, marked `(from "by EOD")`), source space + thread with a quote of the key message, and hard/soft commitment grade.
5. If exporting to Notion: read the database schema, check for existing tasks from the same thread (dedupe), map fields to the real property names.
6. Present the review table; on `apply`, create the pages and return links. Without Notion, deliver the copy-ready checklist grouped by owner.

## Output format

```
Chat action items - <spaces scanned>, window <range> · threads read: <n>

YOURS (<n>)
| # | Task | From | Deadline | Evidence | Grade |
| 1 | Send rollout comms draft | Priya, #launch | Thu (from "before the demo") | "can you own the comms?" → your 👍 | hard |

YOU ASSIGNED (<n>)
| # | Task | Owner | Deadline | Evidence |

SOFT / UNCLAIMED (<n>) - worth pinning down:
- "someone should update the runbook" (#ops, no owner emerged)

Superseded/dropped during scan: <n> (e.g. "never mind" follow-ups)
Say `apply 1,2` to create these in <Notion database> - or take the list as-is.
--- after apply ---
Created: <task → Notion link> ...
```

## Do

- Verify @google-chat and @notion connectors and discover exact tool names first
- Read whole threads before judging any message - the acceptance often arrives as a reaction or a one-word reply
- Quote the evidence message for every extracted task so the user can trust or veto it in one glance
- Resolve relative deadlines ("EOD", "Thursday") against the message timestamp, and mark them as resolved
- Honor later corrections in a thread - the last word wins

## Don't

- Don't post anything into any space while scanning
- Don't turn open questions or ownerless "someone should" messages into assigned tasks - list them as unclaimed
- Don't miss reaction-based acceptances (👍 on a request = yes) or count a joke reaction as one when the thread context says banter
- Don't duplicate tasks already exported from the same thread in a previous run
- Don't write to Notion before `apply`

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-chat, @notion) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gchat-action-items or describe the task from the skill description.

skills.aiInstallPrompt:
Install the skill 'gchat-digest' by writing the file below.

Universal path: ./skills/gchat-digest/SKILL.md
Grok path: ~/.grok/skills/gchat-digest/SKILL.md
Cursor path: ~/.cursor/skills/gchat-digest/SKILL.md
Claude path: ~/.claude/skills/gchat-digest/SKILL.md
VS Code agent path: .github/skills/gchat-digest/SKILL.md

Create the folder if needed, then write SKILL.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: gchat-digest
description: >
  Summarize Google Chat spaces. Requires integrations: @google-chat. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: daily. Triggers: "gchat digest", /gchat-digest.
metadata:
  category: google-chat
  type: connector
  cadence: daily
  risk: low
  connectors: google-chat
  integrations: @google-chat
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gchat-digest

Summarize Google Chat spaces.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

The user was heads-down (or off) while their Google Chat spaces kept moving - hundreds of messages across team spaces and DMs. This is the catch-up briefing: what was decided, what's still being argued, where the user was @mentioned or asked something, and which threads they can safely ignore. Run each morning or after any half-day away.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Window (default: since yesterday evening / last 24h; after leave, the away dates)
- Space priorities: which spaces are must-read vs skim vs mute-level noise (bot-heavy alert spaces)
- Include DMs and small group chats? (default: yes - that's where direct asks hide)
- Standing topics to always surface (a launch, an incident, a client name)

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Brief channel-appropriate updates.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-chat** - Google Chat
  - MCP server key: `google-chat`
  - Example tools: `google-chat__list_spaces`, `google-chat__list_messages`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- The digest is **read-only**: no posting, reacting, or replying anywhere while compiling. Messages are not marked read.
- If the user wants a reply sent to something in the digest, draft it and hand off to an `apply`-gated send (see /gchat-send-update) - never reply directly from this skill.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Chat tools, list the user's spaces and DMs, apply the priority map, and pull messages for the window from each in-scope conversation.
2. Reconstruct threads before summarizing - chat meaning lives in reply chains, not single messages. Track each active thread to its current state: resolved (decision made), open (still under discussion), or stalled (question hanging with no reply).
3. Sweep specifically for the user: @mentions, direct questions in DMs and threads they're part of, and their name in discussions they haven't seen. Anything awaiting THEIR answer outranks everything else.
4. Distill each must-read space to what a returning person needs:
   - **Decisions made** - one line each, with who decided ("agreed to postpone launch to Aug 4 - Sam, #launch").
   - **Still open** - the live debates, current positions in half a line.
   - **FYI** - releases, announcements; group bot spam into a count.
5. Include shared artifacts worth opening (docs/files posted with real discussion around them), skip drive-by link drops.
6. Order the digest: (1) things waiting on the user, (2) decisions they should know, (3) open threads by priority space, (4) one-line space roundup. Target: readable in 2 minutes, every item traceable to its space/thread.

## Output format

```
Chat catch-up - <window> · scanned: <k> spaces + <d> DMs, <m> messages → <t> threads

🎯 WAITING ON YOU (<n>)
| Where | Who | The ask | Since |
| #launch (thread) | Priya | "ok to ship without the FR translation?" | yesterday 16:02 |

✅ DECIDED WHILE YOU WERE OUT
- Launch moved to Aug 4 (Sam, #launch) · On-call swap approved (#ops)

🔥 STILL OPEN
- #design: dark-mode default - 2 for, 1 against, no call yet
- DM w/ Tom: budget question circling, needs your number

📎 Worth opening: "Rollout runbook v2" (posted #launch, 6 replies)
📋 Roundup: #random noise (skipped) · #alerts 47 bot messages, 0 human · #sales quiet

Want a reply drafted to any of these? Name it - I'll draft for your approval before anything posts.
```

## Do

- Verify the @google-chat connector and discover exact tool names first
- Reconstruct reply chains; summarizing chat message-by-message produces nonsense
- Put asks aimed at the user first, always, with how long they've been waiting
- Report decisions as decisions ("X was agreed") separately from ongoing debate
- Compress bot/alert spaces to a count unless a human replied to one

## Don't

- Don't post, react, or reply anywhere during the digest run
- Don't present one side of an open debate as the outcome
- Don't quote long exchanges - one line per thread plus where to find it
- Don't skip DMs; direct asks land there more than in spaces
- Don't fabricate activity for a space the connector couldn't read - mark it "couldn't access" instead

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-chat) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **daily**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /gchat-digest`
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gchat-digest or describe the task from the skill description.

  integrations: @google-chat

```markdown
---
name: gchat-send-update
description: >
  Post weekly status to Google Chat space. Requires integrations: @google-chat. Uses search_tool + use_tool. Cadence: weekly. Triggers: "gchat send update", /gchat-send-update.
metadata:
  category: google-chat
  type: connector
  cadence: weekly
  risk: medium
  connectors: google-chat
  integrations: @google-chat
allowed-tools: search_tool, use_tool
---
# /gchat-send-update

Post weekly status to Google Chat space.

## Works with

Use this as a portable `SKILL.md` in Grok, Claude, Cursor, ChatGPT, Codex, VS Code agent skills, Gemini, or any AI workspace that supports reusable markdown instructions. If a tool does not support slash commands, paste the task and ask the AI to follow this skill.

## When to use

Friday afternoon (or standup time) and the weekly status is due in the team's Chat space. The user tells the AI what happened - or points at the week's artifacts - and gets a crisp, channel-appropriate status message drafted, reviewed, and only then posted to the right space. The skill's whole personality: short, scannable, and never posted without an explicit go.

## Inputs to gather

Check context first; only ask if missing:

- Target space (exact name - verify it exists and the user means #team-alpha, not #team-alpha-social) and whether it's a threaded space (post as new thread vs reply into a standing "weekly updates" thread)
- The raw material: the user's bullet points, or permission to draw from context in this session (recent digests, task lists)
- The team's update format if one exists (many teams run "Done / Next / Blockers" or traffic-light) - match it; otherwise default to Done / Next / Blockers
- Any @mentions needed (thanking someone, flagging a blocker owner) - each one pings a person, so they must be deliberate

## Tone & voice

- **Tone:** Brief channel-appropriate updates.
- **Audience:** Infer from user context.

## Connector access (required)

This skill **cannot run from text alone** - it must call live integrations.
Before any workflow step:

1. Check your AI tool's connector settings and confirm each integration below is enabled and authenticated (Grok: run `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: enable the app in settings · other tools: ask your AI what app connections it has).
2. Use `search_tool` to discover tools for each connector (search by app name).
3. Call integrations with `use_tool` and fully-qualified names (`server__tool_name`).
4. Connector tool names vary by AI tool. Discover the available tools first, then call the exact tool name exposed in the current session.

### Required integrations

- **@google-chat** - Google Chat
  - MCP server key: `google-chat`
  - Example tools: `google-chat__list_spaces`, `google-chat__list_messages`

### If a connector is missing

Stop immediately. Tell the user:

```
This skill needs <@mention> enabled.
1. Open your AI tool's connector/integration settings (Grok: `/mcps` · Claude: Settings → Connectors · Cursor: Settings → MCP · ChatGPT: app settings)
2. Enable and authenticate <app>
3. Refresh your tool list, then run this skill again
```

Do **not** pretend you accessed Gmail/Canva/Drive without a successful `use_tool` call.

## Safety

- **Propose before write**: show a review table; wait for user to say `apply`, `apply all`, or `apply 1,3`.
- Never delete, send, or publish without explicit confirmation.
- Summarize every action taken at the end of the run so the user can review it.

## Workflow

1. Discover the Chat tools and resolve the target space by listing spaces and matching the name - confirm with the user if more than one plausible match exists. Posting status to the wrong space is this skill's embarrassing failure mode; a misfired @mention is its rude one.
2. If the team posts updates into a recurring thread, find the latest one; also skim the user's previous update (if visible) so this week's "Next" is consistent with last week's promises - call out anything promised last week that isn't in this week's Done or Blockers.
3. Draft in the team's format (default Done / Next / Blockers):
   - **Done**: outcomes, not activity - "shipped v2 login flow" beats "worked on login".
   - **Next**: max 3 items; a list of 8 is a plan, not a status.
   - **Blockers**: name what's blocked, since when, and who can unblock - with an @mention only if the user approves pinging them.
   - Chat-native formatting: bold section labels, hyphen bullets, no headers/walls of text; whole message readable without scrolling on a laptop.
4. Include links to artifacts (docs, dashboards) rather than restating their contents.
5. Show the exact final message + destination + thread placement + every @mention for review. Post only after `apply` - never on a vague "looks good" if @mentions changed after the last review.
6. Post, confirm with a link to the message, and note it for next week's consistency check.

## Output format

```
Ready to post → space: #<name> (<as new thread | reply into "Weekly updates" thread>)
@mentions that will ping: <@Tom (blocker owner)> - confirm ok

------------------------------------------
*Weekly update - <team/project>, w/e <date>*
*Done:*
- Shipped v2 login flow (docs: <link>)
- Closed 14 support backlog tickets
*Next:*
- Migration dry-run Tue · Draft Q3 OKRs
*Blockers:*
- Waiting on security review since Mon - @Tom, anything I can do to help it along?
------------------------------------------

Consistency note: last week you promised the dry-run "this week" - it's now in Next; want a line acknowledging the slip?
Say `apply` to post. Nothing sends until you do.
--- after apply ---
Posted to #<space> → <message link>.
```

## Do

- Verify the @google-chat connector and discover exact tool names first
- Resolve and confirm the exact space before drafting; verify thread placement for teams that keep update threads
- Write Done items as outcomes with links, and keep the whole update under ~12 lines
- Surface last week's unfulfilled promises so the user decides how to handle them - never hide the slip yourself
- Re-show the message for a fresh `apply` if anything (especially @mentions) changed after review

## Don't

- Don't post without the explicit `apply` - a status update in Chat is instantly public to the whole space
- Don't @mention anyone the user didn't explicitly approve
- Don't pad with corporate filler ("continued alignment on synergies") - every line earns its place
- Don't paste doc contents into the message; link them
- Don't post to a best-guess space when the name match was ambiguous - ask

## Quality check (before responding)

- [ ] Output matches requested tone and channel
- [ ] No placeholder brackets left unfilled (use sensible defaults or ask)
- [ ] User can copy-paste the result immediately
- [ ] Required integrations (@google-chat) verified in your AI tool's connector settings before `use_tool` calls
- [ ] Connector actions proposed, not auto-applied (unless read-only)

## Schedule

Recommended cadence: **weekly**

Example: `/loop 7d Run /gchat-send-update`
```

After writing the file, confirm it exists. The user can then run /gchat-send-update or describe the task from the skill description.
